AC1.proof: raw/live-verify.txt is sanitized (host as <demo-host>, credentials marked redacted; secret-scan found no bare IPv4, no credential-in-URL, no root/root or password= patterns). It substantiates the LIVE finding: DomainService.ListComponents discovered StateControl.relay0:0/0:1 and TelemetryControl.{0,1,2} on the only relay/telemetry-bearing device (generic driver); StateControl SetState/GetCurrentState/GetDefaultState return INTERNAL unresolved-reference on both relays; TagAndTrack ListTrackers returns NOT_FOUND on the device, every TelemetryControl endpoint, and the video source; ConfigurationManager.GetRevisionInfo works (461 revisions) while SetRevision is described as a destructive whole-config restore left pending and not probed as a mutation. Stand is not reachable from the verifier, so the sanitized artifact is treated as the evidence per spec.

AC2.proof: docs/api-audit/mcp-corpus/api_methods.json: StateControlService.SetState and TagAndTrackService.SetMode/FollowTrack/MoveToCoords are tested-warn-fixture-needed; ConfigurationManager.SetRevision stays pending. JSON diff vs HEAD shows exactly those 4 status flips (pending -> tested-warn-fixture-needed) and no other ledger row touched. Aggregate totals: 283 tested-pass (260 tested-pass + 21 tested-pass-safe-record + 2 tested-pass-empty) / 20 pending / 58 tested-warn-fixture-needed = 361. The 6 TelemetryService methods Move/GoPreset/ReleaseSessionId/KeepAlive/SetPreset2/RemovePreset remain tested-pass after the duplicate-key dedup (the phase-33 tested-pass entries survive; only the dead-shadowed phase-8 fixture-warn duplicates were removed from the restamp source). Restamp dry-run reports 0 method(s) restamped (idempotent). Coverage doc headline (283/20/58) + phase-47 Item 10ah note present; README and STATUS coverage lines read 283/20/58.

AC3.proof: python3.12 -m pytest tools/tests/ -q -> 969 passed. uvx ruff check tools/axxon_corpus_restamp.py -> All checks passed (F601 duplicate-key warnings cleared). Changed tracked files are limited to README.md, STATUS.md, the coverage doc, api_methods.json, and the corpus restamp tool; no production MCP runtime module was modified, so the green suite is a clean regression guard.

secret_scan: clean: live-verify.txt and all changed tracked files contain no bare IPv4, no demo host IP, no root/root or password-value patterns; the only 'password' token is benign prose in STATUS.md describing a credential-change workflow
notes: Minor non-blocking prose drift: README line 14 and STATUS line 4 narrative still say '54 fixture-warn are live-probed' while the headline correctly reads 58 fixture-warn.
